findings.explanation: The balance mapping is decremented after the external token.transfer call, allowing a reentrant caller to re-enter withdraw and drain the vault before state is updated.
setTreasury has no access control, allowing any caller to redirect protocol fee collection to an arbitrary address.
discountBps is not capped at 10000, so a market creator can set a 100%+ discount making effectivePrice zero, enabling buyers to drain tokens for free.
withdrawTo makes an external call before zeroing deposits[msg.sender] without a reentrancy guard, allowing a malicious recipient to re-enter and drain the vault.
setRewardPerBlock has no access control, allowing any caller to arbitrarily inflate or zero out the reward rate, manipulating yields for all stakers.

If totalStaked is zero when _updateRewards is called (e.g., during the first stake call before totalStaked is incremented), the division (stakedBalance * 1e18) / totalStaked will revert, permanently bricking stake().
There is no nonce or used-signature tracking. A valid signed transfer can be replayed repeatedly until the signer's token allowance or balance is exhausted.
setPrice has no onlyAdmin modifier, allowing any caller to set the sale price to zero or an arbitrary value and buy tokens for free.
transferAdmin has no access control, allowing anyone to immediately seize admin privileges including closeSale, setCap, and withdrawPayments.
